_sources.ovx: CBOE CDN OVX_History.csv - D0 uses Feb 27 close (Feb 28 = Saturday); D7/D8 carry D6 (weekend, market closed); D10 live via /api/cboe
_sources.vxeem: CBOE CDN VXEEM_History.csv - all D0-D9 confirmed from live data; D7/D8 carry D6 (weekend); D10 live via /api/cboe
_sources.scenarios: D10-D12 confirmed from banker-conflict.json; all prior days estimated from narrative context

days.note: Mon - First trading day after Jun 5 US-Iran drone/radar exchange (US shot down 4 Iranian drones, struck radar sites; Iran fired 7 ballistic missiles at Kuwait/Bahrain, 6 intercepted). Market reopens with elevated tail risk. Oil stays high; tail 47%.

Tue - Iran shoots down US Army Apache helicopter over Strait of Hormuz; US issues stern warning. First US aircraft loss over Hormuz. Trump ordered 49-Tomahawk response. Escalation peak approaching. Tail 50%.

Wed - US retaliates: 49 Tomahawk missiles strike targets including within 40 miles of Tehran ('proportional response' to Apache shootdown). Largest US strike on Iranian territory since conflict began. Tail 50%. Brent surges on escalation.
Thu - No immediate Iranian response to Tomahawk strikes. Tensions plateau after D100 peak. Both sides absorbing escalation; Pakistan backchannel intensifying. Tail 48% (slight easing).

Fri - MAJOR DE-ESCALATION: Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif announces US and Iran have reached 'final, agreed upon text' of deal (Washington Post). Mediators working on next steps; deal to be signed in coming days. Brent falls sharply on deal signal. Tail 37%.
Sat - Market closed; deal details being finalised; signing date set for Friday June 19 in Switzerland; scenarios carried from D102.

Sun - DEAL ANNOUNCED: Trump declares deal complete on social media; US lifts naval blockade on Iranian ports effective immediately; Hormuz to reopen after signing June 19 in Switzerland as 'permanently toll free.' Iran deputy FM confirms MOU text finalised. Brent -5% to $83.17 in Asian futures (CNN Jun 14). Tail collapses to 20%.

Mon - Deal reaction: Brent -4.7% to $83.17 close / $84.62 intraday (Fortune Jun 15). Oil at three-month lows. Iran deputy FM: nuclear talks begin only after frozen funds released. Iran 'differing versions' of MOU text (Fortune Jun 14). Markets price in deal but residual implementation risk. Tail 15%.

Tue - Brent $81.55 (Fortune morning 8:30am; fifth straight session lower from D93 $98.57 peak). Signing preparations for Jun 19 in Switzerland. Iran implementation disputes flagged but deal track intact. iTraxx Asia IG ~142bp est.; EM FX broadly stronger. Tail 15%.

Wed - Brent $79.45 (TradingEconomics Jun 17, +0.6% minor bounce; -19% from D93 $98.57 peak). WTI $75.47; TTF €41.12 (-1.6%). At three-month lows. 2 days to MOU signing. Iran differing MOU versions + nuclear sequencing unresolved; deputy FM frozen-funds condition. Deal fragile but signing track intact. Tail 15%.

Thu - MOU digitally pre-signed at G7 Versailles (Jun 18, Haaretz). Formal ceremony at Burgenstock tomorrow. Brent $79.34 (TradingEconomics, -0.27%); WTI $76.43 (-0.47%); TTF €41.33 (-1.39%). Markets pricing deal confirmation. Tail 12%.

Fri - MOU formally signed Burgenstock, Switzerland — formal ceremony complete. Phase 2 nuclear talks begin immediately. US markets closed Juneteenth; Brent $79.34 carried (Jun 18 close). Escalation lowered to 2 (Elevated). Tail 10%.
